ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR RESPONDENT CALIFORNIA PUBLIC UTILITIES COMMISSION'S DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/CERTIFICATION HEARING SET FOR JAN-25-2016 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, RESPONDENT CALIFORNIA PUBLIC UTILITIES COMMISSION'S DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/PROHIBITION/CERTIFICATION - 1) THE ISSUE OF EXHAUSTION OF ADMINISTRATIVE REMEDIES - THE COURT FINDS THAT PLAINTIFF'S FAILURE TO EXHAUST ADMINISTRATIVE REMEDIES DOES NOT BAR PLAINTIFF'S ACTION BROUGHT PURSUANT TO THE PUBLIC RELATIONS ACT AND FURTHER ATTEMPTS BY INVOKING ADMINISTRATIVE REMEDIES WOULD BE FUTILE. THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED ON THIS GROUND. 2) THE ISSUE OF THE GOVERNMENT CODE SECTION 995 CLAIM - THE DEMURRER TO THIS CLAIM IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. 3) THE ISSUE OF JURISDICTION - THE COURT FINDS THAT THE SUPERIOR COURT IS NOT BARRED FROM JURISDICTION IN THE INSTANT CASE. THE COURT WILL PREPARE AN ORDER. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976. (302/EG)
ALTERNATIVE WRIT A147570 DIV 2

calendar.matters:
Respondent California Public Utilities Commission'S Demurrer To Petition For Writ Of Mandate/ Prohibition/Certification
Off Calendar Jan-25-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Michael Aguirre of Aguirre & Severson LLP telephone number 619-876-5364 for Plaintiff.
Maria Severson of Aguirre & Severson LLP telephone number 619-876-5364 appeared by telephone for Plaintiff.
Mitchell Shapson of State of California Public Uitilities Commission Legal Division telephone number 415-703-2727 for Defendant.
Jonathan Koltz of State of California Public Uitilities Commission Legal Division telephone number 415-703-2760 for Defendant.

Counsel reported that there is no change in the posture of the case and no agreement as to the production of documents. The Court set forth its understanding of the facts/allegations in the case. The parties presented oral argument to support their positions. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court ruled as follows: In re: Respondent California Public Utilities Commission's Demurrer To Petition For Writ Of Mandate/Prohibition/Certification
1) The issue of exhaustion of administrative remedies - The Court finds that Plaintiff's failure to exhaust administrative remedies does not bar plaintiff's action brought pursuant to the Public Relations Act and further attempts by invoking administrative remedies would be futile. The demurrer is overruled on this ground. 2) The issue of the Government Code section 995 Claim - The demurrer to this claim is sustained without leave to amend. 3) The issue of jurisdiction - The Court finds that the Superior Court is not barred from jurisdiction in the instant case. The Court will prepare an order.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/EG)

DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Not reported. (302/EG)

Mitchell Shapson and Jonathan Koltz of Public Uitilities Commission Legal Division telephone number 415-703-2760 for Respondent.

The parties reported that there is no agreement on the release of any additional documents. The matter was argued and the parties addressed the issue of jurisdiction.
